text:
Josh Kerr, the athlete who clinched a silver medal in the Men's 1500m at the Paris Olympics, appeared onGood Morning Britainon Friday. Despite narrowly missing out on gold, Josh improved upon his previous bronze medal achievement.

During his video link interview with hosts Kate Garraway and Rob Rinder, he discussed his Olympic experience. However, when Kate mentioned she'd heard that Josh prefers not to have his family at races due to the added pressure, he was quick to set the record straight.

Kate asked, "I know you generally - well you've been reported as saying this, you might tell me different - you generally are not sure about having your mum and dad there when you race as it almost throws you a little bit. You know they're supporting you but it puts an extra pressure. They were there (in Paris) and I believe you didn't know they were there until afterwards. Is that right?"

READ MORE:ITV Good Morning Britain fans rush to 'switch off' as they issue complaint over Katie Price coverage

MORE TV:Loose Women's Kaye Adams faces audience backlash after brazen holiday statement

To which Josh responded with a hint of confusion, "Not too sure where that info's come from but I love it when the family's there, I live for that kind of pressure, you know they've been there throughout my whole career in the stands cheering me on and it means the world to me to have the people that got me there to be in the stands.

"And I think I realised reasonably quickly when I was talking the big game of going after an Olympic gold medal people were like 'oh ok we can just buy tickets and sit in the stadium and watch you' so I had a good amount of support there," reportsthe Mirror.

Earlier in the programme, viewers expressed their anger, threatening to 'switch off' over the coverage of Katie Price's arrest. The former glamour model, and mother-of-five, was arrested following the issuance of a warrant as she failed to make a court appearance last month.

However, complaints arose due to the extensive focus on this story rather than on the less prominently covered case of Ricky Jones. Confirmation has been received from the Metropolitan Police regarding the arrest of a man in his fifties for serious allegations of inciting murder and an offence under the Public Order Act.

The row ignited whenLabourcouncillor Ricky Jones was seen in a video seeming to endorse violence by speaking of slashing the throats of far-Right protesters. TheLabour Partypromptly distanced itself from Mr Jones, relieving him of his position in light of the controversial video.

GMB is on ITV weekdays at 6am

title: GMB's Kate Garraway corrected by Olympic medallist after interview blunder  | Wales Online
author: Karen Price
description: Good Morning Britain presenter Kate Garraway was left red-faced during Friday's edition of the ITV show after she was corrected by a Team GB Olympian during a live interview
